title: actinodaphne engleriana
intro: Actinodaphne engleriana, also known as the Korean boxwood or Korean box, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae. It is native to China and Korea, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and treating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Actinodaphne engleriana are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to have antiseptic and antispasmodic properties.
